summary: Draft candidate record only. The Session Manager watchdog timeout pair now has baseline existence on Win25H2Clean, exact ntoskrnl string hits plus current-build Ghidra fallback artifacts, repo-side PoFx pseudocode that ties the pair to directed power watchdog timeout globals, and a successful reboot-verified boot trace baseline. The pair is strong enough to stay active as a candidate lane, but there is still no shipped app mapping and no direct live read of the exact values yet.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Successful run: watchdog-timeouts-boottrace-20260328-090631. Boot cycle advanced from 2026-03-25T14:14:21.5000000+03:00 to 2026-03-28T09:09:02.5000000+03:00 and preserved WatchdogResumeTimeout = 120 plus WatchdogSleepTimeout = 300 after boot.
validation_proof.notes: This proof anchors the pair to a reboot-verified baseline on Win25H2Clean. It is enough to confirm the candidate surface exists and survives boot, but not enough to claim a shipped app mapping or a validated non-default profile.

setting.name: Session Manager Watchdog Timeouts
setting.casual_explanation: These values appear to control watchdog timeout behavior around directed power transitions. They are not normal user-facing power options and are being tracked as a low-level research lane rather than a supported tweak.
setting.why_people_change_it: Advanced researchers may look at these values when investigating resume and sleep watchdog behavior, power transition timing, or kernel power management internals.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The pair is kernel-adjacent and boot-relevant. The current evidence shows that the values exist and participate in the power-management path, but the repo still does not have a safe shipped mapping or a validated non-default profile for end users.
setting.professional_notes: The tracked values are HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Power\WatchdogResumeTimeout and WatchdogSleepTimeout. A clean baseline export on Win25H2Clean confirmed 120 and 300 respectively, with adjacent PowerSettingProfile = 0. The current-build ntoskrnl.exe string passes found exact hits for both names, and the improved Ghidra fallback preserved reviewable artifacts even when natural function boundaries were weak. Repo-side PoFxInitPowerManagement pseudocode then linked the pair to PopFxDirectedPowerUpTimeoutMs and PopFxDirectedPowerDownTimeoutMs. A successful host-driven boot trace on baseline-20260327-regprobe-visible-shell-stable preserved the same 120/300 values after reboot and captured the ETL to host staging. That is enough to keep the candidate active, but not enough to publish a shipped preset or app mapping.

allowed_values.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline
allowed_values.meaning: The clean VM baseline currently exposes WatchdogResumeTimeout = 120 and WatchdogSleepTimeout = 300.

allowed_values.label: Undocumented custom DWORD pair
allowed_values.meaning: The evidence so far only proves that the pair exists and flows through the power-management path. A safe non-default profile is not validated yet.

targets.notes: Keep the pair together. The current evidence is strongest when WatchdogResumeTimeout and WatchdogSleepTimeout are treated as one lane.

app_current_implementation.provider_source: not currently shipped in the app
app_current_implementation.notes: There is no current RegProbe provider or UI mapping for this candidate. The pair is still in research-only intake.

states.rationale: Leave the currently observed watchdog timeout pair unchanged until a stronger supported mapping exists.

Keeps the lane documentation-only until a supported mapping exists.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean Session Manager Power baseline export
evidence.summary: A clean VM baseline export confirmed WatchdogResumeTimeout = 120 and WatchdogSleepTimeout = 300 under Session Manager\Power, with adjacent PowerSettingProfile = 0.

evidence.title: Current-build ntoskrnl Ghidra fallback for the watchdog timeout pair
evidence.summary: The current-build ntoskrnl.exe pass found exact string hits for WatchdogResumeTimeout and WatchdogSleepTimeout and preserved reviewable fallback artifacts for the next-gate watchdog lane.

evidence.title: Repo PoFxInitPowerManagement pseudocode lead
evidence.summary: The repo-side pseudocode ties PopWatchdogResumeTimeout and PopWatchdogSleepTimeout to the directed power-up and directed power-down watchdog timeout globals, giving the pair a subsystem-level semantic lead.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean reboot-verified watchdog timeout boot trace
evidence.summary: A successful host-driven boot trace on the current visible-shell baseline confirmed a real reboot cycle, preserved the same 120/300 values after boot, and kept shell health intact.

evidence.title: Kernel power next-gate intake summary
evidence.summary: The 2026-03-28 intake review ranked the watchdog pair above the Executive worker-thread values because the baseline existence, exact string hits, and Ghidra artifacts were already stronger.

decision.why: The watchdog timeout pair now has cross-layer evidence: a clean baseline export, exact current-build ntoskrnl string and Ghidra fallback artifacts, repo-side PoFx pseudocode, and a successful reboot-verified boot trace that preserved the same values after boot. That is strong enough to keep the pair active as a candidate record, but there is still no shipped app mapping, no direct live read of the exact values, and no validated non-default profile.
blocking_issues: No shipped RegProbe provider or UI mapping exists for the watchdog timeout pair yet.
The current semantic lead still depends on repo-side pseudocode plus forced-boundary Ghidra review rather than a primary Microsoft registry contract.
The lane still lacks a direct live read of WatchdogResumeTimeout and WatchdogSleepTimeout.

open_questions: Can the exact watchdog pair be captured in a direct runtime read rather than only a reboot-verified boot trace baseline?
Does a better symbol or binary target produce a cleaner current-build code path than the current forced-boundary ntoskrnl fallback?
Should this pair ever become a shipped tweak, or should it stay documentation-only even if semantics converge?

tested_on.notes: Baseline export and successful host-driven boot trace both ran on baseline-20260327-regprobe-visible-shell-stable.
